question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a Juju charm can observe, organized by category:

---

## Lifecycle Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `install` | Fired once when the charm is first installed |
| `start` | Fired after `install` (or after upgrade); charm should start services |
| `stop` | Fired when the unit is being removed |
| `remove` | Fired just before the unit is finally removed |
| `config-changed` | Fired whenever charm configuration changes (also after `install` and `upgrade-charm`) |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fired when a new version of the charm is deployed |
| `update-status` | Fired periodically (default every 5 minutes) for status polling |
| `collect-app-status` | Fired to collect application-level status (ops v2+) |
| `collect-unit-status` | Fired to collect unit-level status (ops v2+) |

---

## Relation Hooks

These fire for every relation the charm is a member of (one set per relation endpoint):

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `<relation>-relation-created` | A new relation has been created |
| `<relation>-relation-joined` | A remote unit has joined the relation |
| `<relation>-relation-changed` | Remote unit's relation data has changed |
| `<relation>-relation-departed` | A remote unit is leaving the relation |
| `<relation>-relation-broken` | The relation itself is being removed |

---

## Storage Hooks

One set per storage defined in `metadata.yaml`:

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | A storage instance has been attached to the unit |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | A storage instance is about to be detached |

---

## Workload Hooks (Kubernetes / Pebble)

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | The Pebble API in a container is ready |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | A custom Pebble notice has been fired |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | A Pebble health check has failed |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | A previously failed Pebble health check has recovered |

---

## Secret Hooks (Juju 3.x+)

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `secret-changed` | A secret this unit tracks has a new revision available |
| `secret-expired` | A secret's expiry time has been reached |
| `secret-rotate` | The secret owner is notified it's time to rotate a secret |
| `secret-remove` | An old secret revision can now be safely removed |

---

## Leader Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `leader-elected` | This unit has become the application leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | The leader has written to leader settings (fired on non-leader units) |

---

## Pre/Post Series Upgrade (deprecated in Juju 3.x)

| Hook | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before an OS series upgrade |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after an OS series upgrade |

---

### Summary Count
- **9** core lifecycle hooks
- **5** relation hooks (× number of endpoints)
- **2** storage hooks (× number of storage definitions)
- **4** workload/Pebble hooks (× number of containers)
- **4** secret hooks
- **2** leader hooks
- **2** series upgrade hooks (legacy)

In the **ops library**, each of these maps to a corresponding Python event class (e.g., `InstallEvent`, `RelationChangedEvent`, `PebbleReadyEvent`, `SecretChangedEvent`, etc.) that you observe via `self.framework.observe(self.on.<event>, self._handler)`.
